vocab.meaning: tốn rất nhiều tiền, làm khánh kiệt túi tiền
vocab.example: I only buy famous sneakers when they are on sale, as I don't want to break the bank for a single pair.

vocab.meaning: đang rất thịnh hành, là mốt
vocab.example: Designer chunky boots are all the rage right now, but I prefer more classic, understated styles.

vocab.meaning: trả giá quá đắt cho một món đồ
vocab.example: Many people are willing to pay through the nose for limited-edition brands just for the prestige.
These high-end running shoes were expensive, but given their comfort, they were worth every penny.

vocab.meaning: có vẻ ngoài phù hợp (với hoàn cảnh/địa vị)
vocab.example: I usually buy branded dress shoes for work because they really help me look the part during business meetings.

vocab.word: cost an arm and a leg
vocab.example: Luxury brands often cost an arm and a leg, so I reserve those purchases for special milestones.

vocab.meaning: làm việc sơ sài, đốt cháy giai đoạn (thường để nói về chất lượng hàng rẻ tiền)
vocab.example: Unlike budget footwear manufacturers who cut corners, famous brands usually maintain high standards of production.

vocab.meaning: nạn nhân thời trang (người luôn chạy theo mốt một cách mù quáng)
vocab.example: I try not to be a fashion victim by buying every new branded release regardless of whether it suits me.
The reason I stick to this specific Italian brand is that their footwear always fits like a glove.

vocab.meaning: đua đòi, cố gắng bằng bạn bằng bè
vocab.example: I don't buy famous brands just to keep up with the Joneses; I genuinely value the quality they provide.
